steps.explanation: Express the perimeter of the base. Width = x, length = 2.5x. Perimeter of a rectangle = 2(length + width) = 2(2.5x + x) = 2(3.5x) = 7x inches.
Apply the restriction: perimeter + height ≤ 130. With height = 60: 7x + 60 ≤ 130.
Solve for x: subtract 60 from both sides to get 7x ≤ 70, then divide by 7 to get x ≤ 10. Since width must be positive, the complete inequality is 0 < x ≤ 10.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: 0 < x ≤ 10. This matches the derived inequality exactly. That confirms A is correct.
  • B: 0 < x ≤ 11⅔. This would follow from 6x + 60 ≤ 130, which uses the wrong perimeter formula. Eliminated.
  • C: 0 < x ≤ 17.5. This would follow from 3.5x + 60 ≤ 130, omitting the factor of 2 in the perimeter. Eliminated.
  • D: 0 < x ≤ 20. This would follow from 2x + 60 ≤ 130, using only width instead of the full perimeter. Eliminated.
